People’s desperate situations provide opportunities for God to display His power and glory; He appears for His people when their strength is gone. Unbelief is a sin by which people greatly dishonor and displease God, and deprive themselves of the blessings He intended for them. This will be the fate of those who do not believe in the promise of eternal life; they will see it from afar but never experience it. However, no temporary rescues or kindnesses will ultimately benefit sinners unless they are led to repentance by the goodness of God.

God can, when He chooses, make even the strongest heart tremble; and for those who do not fear God, He can make them fear at the rustling of a leaf. Providence arranged it so that the lepers arrived just as the Syrians fled. Their consciences told them that harm would come to them if they only looked out for themselves. Natural humanity and fear of punishment are powerful restraints on the selfishness of ungodly people. These feelings help maintain order and kindness in the world; but those who have discovered Christ’s boundless riches will not delay long in reporting the good news to others. From love for Him, not from selfish feelings, they will gladly share their earthly possessions with their brothers and sisters.

Here we see Israel's needs met in a way they scarcely imagined, which should encourage us to depend on God’s power and goodness even in our most difficult circumstances. We can safely rely on God’s promise, for no word of His will fail. The nobleman who questioned the truth of Elisha’s words saw the abundance, silencing and shaming his unbelief, and recognized his own foolishness; but he did not partake of the abundance he saw. Those who believe that God's promises will disappoint them will justly find that the world’s promises fail them. Let us learn how deeply God resents distrust of His power, providence, and promise: how uncertain life is, and its pleasures: how certain God’s threats are, and how sure they are to come upon the guilty. May God help us to examine whether we are exposed to His threats or included in His promises.
